question: Does the generated image include the woman from the reference, wearing a grey blazer, white t-shirt, black pants, and white sneakers?
Does the generated image include the man from the reference, wearing glasses and a dark coat?
Is the dog in the image a black and white Border Collie matching the reference animal?
Is the suitcase a black, hard-shell model matching the reference object?
Is the background clearly recognizable as the high-speed rail station hall provided in the reference image?
Is the woman positioned in the right foreground of the image?
Is the man positioned in the left mid-ground, appearing further back than the woman?
Is the Border Collie positioned between the man and the woman, toward the front?
Is the woman's body oriented as if walking toward the right, with her head turned back looking at the camera?
Is the man holding a leash with his left hand?
Does the leash depict a natural slack curve rather than a straight, stiff line?
Does the leash connect cleanly between the man's hand and the dog without floating endpoints or clipping?
Is the woman pulling the suitcase with her right hand?
Are all four legs of the dog grounded on the floor (not floating)?
Are the suitcase wheels touching the ground with slight motion blur applied along the walking direction?
Are there realistic contact shadows and consistent shadow directions for the people, dog, and suitcase?
Are there subtle reflections of the subjects on the floor (visible but not mirror-strong)?
Is the overall color tone unified to match the station's cool-toned lighting?
Have the passersby from the original background image been completely removed?
Are there any visible ghosting artifacts, smudge marks, or unnatural seams where the background was edited or subjects composited?
Are there any severe anatomical distortions (e.g., extra limbs, malformed hands/paws) on the people or dog?
